summary: American playwright, member of the Group Theatre. Best known for Waiting for Lefty (cab driver strike, Agate's chant "STRIKE!"), Awake and Sing! (Berger family in the Bronx, Jacob, Bessie, Hennie), and Golden Boy (boxer Joe Bonaparte). Named names to HUAC with Elia Kazan. Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on him.

works.name: Waiting for Lefty
works.description: Play (1935) about cab drivers demanding a strike. Seven vignettes separated by blackouts. Ends with Agate's chant "STRIKE!"

clues.clue: Ends with Agate shouting "WE'RE STORMBIRDS OF THE WORKING-CLASS" — cab drivers chant "STRIKE, STRIKE, STRIKE!!!" after Lefty's body is found
examples: Agate shouting, 'WE'RE STORMBIRDS OF THE WORKING-CLASS'
Agate eludes the boss Harry Fatt and leads angry cab drivers in a strike

clues.clue: Seven vignettes separated by blackouts — Miller punches Fayette after refusing to spy on Dr. Brenner
examples: seven 'vignettes' are separated by blackouts
Miller punches Fayette after refusing to spy on Dr. Brenner

clues.clue: Florrie says "Every princess in the Balkans is wearing a dress like this" when Sid picks her up; Irv yells at daughter
examples: Florrie says, 'Every princess in the Balkans is wearing [a dress] like this'

clues.clue: Stenographer tells actor Phil "one dollar buys nine loaves of bread and one copy of The Communist Manifesto"
examples: a stenographer tells the actor Phil, 'one dollar buys nine loaves of bread and one copy of The Communist Manifesto'

works.name: Awake and Sing!
works.description: Play (1935) about the Berger family in the Bronx. Jacob's Caruso records, Hennie and Moe Axelrod, Bessie's tyranny.

clues.clue: Berger family in the Bronx — Jacob jumps off roof after Bessie smashes his Caruso records; Hennie runs off with Moe Axelrod
examples: Jacob kills himself and Bessie forces Hennie to marry the immigrant boarder Sam
Jacob jumps off a roof after his Enrico Caruso records are smashed by his daughter Bessie Berger

clues.clue: Jacob advises grandson to "fight so life shouldn't be printed on dollar bills"
examples: advises his grandson to 'fight so life shouldn't be printed on dollar bills'

clues.clue: Character proclaims after grandfather's death: "I was born! I swear to God, I'm one week old!"
examples: proclaims that after his grandfather died, 'I was born! I swear to God, I'm one week old!'

clues.clue: Bessie forces Hennie to marry immigrant boarder Sam; Sam's brother joined the Navy and may go to Cuba
examples: Bessie forces Hennie to marry the immigrant boarder Sam
his brother Sam joined the Navy and will probably go to Cuba

clues.clue: Woman tells husband their daughter has never seen a grapefruit — fight over repossessed furniture
examples: a woman tells her husband that their daughter has never even seen a grapefruit

works.name: Golden Boy
works.description: Play about boxer Joe Bonaparte who breaks his hand, ruining his violin career.

clues.clue: Joe Bonaparte breaks his hand, ruining prospects as a violinist — becomes a boxer instead
examples: breaks his hand, ruining his prospects as a violinist, in this author's play about the boxer Joe Bonaparte

works.name: Rocket to the Moon
works.description: Play ending after Cleo Singer rejects both Mr. Prince and Ben Stark, seeking "a whole full world, with all the trimmings."

clues.clue: Cleo Singer says she is looking for "a whole full world, with all the trimmings" — rejects both Mr. Prince and Ben Stark
examples: Cleo Singer says she is looking for 'a whole full world, with all the trimmings'

works.name: Till the Day I Die
works.description: Play about anti-Nazi resistance; contains first use of "male chauvinism." Written to accompany Waiting for Lefty.

clues.clue: About anti-Nazi resistance; Taussig brothers print leaflets about American embargo; contains first use of phrase "male chauvinism"
examples: contains the first use of the phrase 'male chauvinism'
this Group Theatre member

clues.clue: Named names to HUAC with Elia Kazan — career faltered afterward
examples: this playwright's career faltered after he and Elia Kazan named each other to HUAC

clues.clue: Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on him; co-wrote screenplay for Sweet Smell of Success
examples: The Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on this playwright
co-wrote the screenplay for the noir Sweet Smell of Success

clues.clue: Controversially lost 1935 Pulitzer to Zoe Akins's The Old Maid (same year as Awake and Sing!)
examples: this author premiered his play about the Berger family, Awake and Sing!

comprehensive_summary: Clifford Odets is an American playwright and member of the Group Theatre, best known for Waiting for Lefty and Awake and Sing! Waiting for Lefty (1935) consists of seven vignettes about cab drivers separated by blackouts, ending with Agate shouting "WE'RE STORMBIRDS OF THE WORKING-CLASS" as cab drivers chant "STRIKE!" after Lefty's body is found. Awake and Sing! depicts the Berger family in the Bronx, where grandfather Jacob jumps off the roof after his daughter Bessie smashes his Caruso records, and Hennie runs off with Moe Axelrod. Jacob advises his grandson to "fight so life shouldn't be printed on dollar bills." Golden Boy follows boxer Joe Bonaparte, who breaks his hand and ruins his violin career. Rocket to the Moon ends with Cleo Singer seeking "a whole full world, with all the trimmings." Odets named names to HUAC alongside Elia Kazan, and the Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on him.
